summary: The biological mechanisms connecting preterm birth to disrupted nitric oxide signaling via increased asymmetric dimethylarginine (ADMA) provide a solid foundation for predicting early endothelial dysfunction. Despite limited direct longitudinal data, physiological pathways support ADMA as a potential biomarker reflecting enduring vascular vulnerabilities from infancy onward.
